home.title: Weather Forecast Dashboard
home.hero_title: Vietnam Weather Data in Real Time
home.hero_desc: Collect, merge and visualize weather data from Vrain and OpenWeather API/HTML in one dashboard.
home.overview_desc: VN Weather Hub provides an end-to-end pipeline: data collection → data processing (view/merge/clean) → ML training → forecasting. It is designed for both first-time users and fast model iteration.
home.card_api_desc: Fetch near real-time weather signals (temperature, humidity, wind, cloud cover, rainfall...) from OpenWeather to build reliable input datasets.
home.card_vrain_desc: Collect station-level rainfall from Vrain using API, Selenium, or HTML parsing, making the pipeline robust even when website structure changes.
home.card_clean_desc: Preview, download, merge, and clean datasets with practical preprocessing steps (missing values, outliers, normalization, encoding, datetime handling) before training.
home.card_ml_desc: Train ML models (Ensemble/CatBoost/XGBoost/LightGBM/Random Forest), monitor metrics + diagnostics, optimize with Optuna, and run forecasting from file or manual input.
home.modal_crawl_api_li1: Fetch weather data from international APIs (temperature, humidity, wind, rain, etc.).
home.modal_crawl_vrain_api_li1: Fetch rainfall data directly from the API (if provided by Vrain).
home.modal_crawl_vrain_api_li2: Stable, less dependent on HTML interface.
home.modal_crawl_selenium_li1: Use Selenium to control the browser and read the Vrain interface.
home.modal_crawl_selenium_li2: Suitable for collecting multiple provinces/stations simultaneously.
home.modal_crawl_html_li1: Parse HTML and use regex to extract rainfall data for each station.
home.modal_crawl_html_li2: Suitable when fine-tuning HTML parsing logic.
home.modal_help_sub: A practical onboarding flow from data collection to final prediction, including what to check at each step.
home.modal_help_step1_li2: Choose one of 4 sources based on your scenario: OpenWeather API, Vrain API, Vrain Selenium, or Vrain HTML.
home.modal_help_step1_li3: The system executes scripts and streams real-time logs so you can track progress and failures.
home.modal_help_step1_li4_pre: When done, the data file (CSV/Excel) will appear in the
home.modal_help_step2_li2_view_desc: preview data tables (with pagination) to verify schema and key columns.
home.modal_help_step2_li2_dl_desc: save files locally for offline validation.
home.modal_help_step2_li3_desc: combine multiple files into a unified dataset for more stable training.
home.modal_help_step2_li4_desc: clean and standardize data — missing values, outliers, normalization, encoding, datetime handling...
home.modal_help_step3_li2: Select a cleaned dataset, configure model in Quick/Advanced mode, and optionally run Optuna for tuning.
home.modal_help_step3_li3_post: — monitor the progress bar and real-time logs.
home.modal_help_step3_li4: Read results in this order: TEST metrics → VALID metrics → diagnostics (overfit/underfit).
home.modal_help_step4_li2: Run forecast from existing dataset or upload CSV/Excel for batch prediction.
home.modal_help_step4_li3: Manual forecast for single-point input: provide weather parameters (temperature, humidity, wind, pressure...).
home.modal_help_step4_li4: Results are shown in preview and full output is saved at WeatherForcast/predictions.csv.
home.modal_intro_sub: An all-in-one platform for rainfall/weather tasks: multi-source collection, data processing, ML training, and prediction in a single interface.
home.modal_intro_what_desc: is a Django-based web system for the full data-to-model workflow: weather data collection, preprocessing, training, evaluation, and rainfall forecasting with Machine Learning.
home.modal_intro_feature1_desc: 4 sources: OpenWeather API, Vrain API, Vrain Selenium, Vrain HTML Parser.
home.modal_intro_feature2_desc: view, download, merge, and clean data directly in the web UI.
home.modal_intro_feature3_desc: supports multiple model types with Quick/Advanced config and Optuna hyperparameter tuning.
home.modal_intro_feature4_desc: forecast from existing dataset, uploaded files, or manual single-point input.
home.modal_intro_feature5_desc: register, login, forgot password (OTP via email).
home.modal_intro_step1_desc: run crawling from one of 4 sources → output files are stored in output/.
home.modal_intro_step2_desc: (optional): combine multiple files into one unified dataset.
home.modal_intro_step3_desc: (optional): clean and standardize data before training for better model quality.
home.modal_intro_step4_desc: select cleaned dataset → configure model → train, monitor logs, and evaluate metrics.
home.modal_intro_step5_desc: use the latest trained model to forecast and export final outputs.
home.modal_intro_source1: OpenWeather: temperature, humidity, wind speed, cloud cover, rainfall…
home.modal_intro_source2: Vrain: detailed rainfall data by monitoring station across Vietnam.
home.modal_intro_quickstart_li2: Select a data source and monitor logs until completion.
home.modal_intro_quickstart_li3_post: to inspect, merge, and clean datasets.
home.modal_intro_quickstart_li4_post: to train and evaluate model quality.
home.modal_intro_quickstart_li5_post: to run forecasting and get result files.
home.footer: Weather Forecast Project — Built with Django

register.title: Create Account
register.subtitle: Create a new account to access all features
register.email_hint: Use a real email (Gmail, Yahoo, Outlook...) to receive verification code. No accented characters.

forgot_password.title: Forgot Password
forgot_password.subtitle: Don't worry! We'll send you password reset instructions via email.
forgot_password.email_hint: We'll send a password reset link to this email

reset_password.title: Set New Password
reset_password.subtitle: Enter a new password for your account
reset_password.new_password_hint: Minimum 8 characters including letters and numbers
reset_password.invalid_message: This password reset link is no longer valid. Please request a new one.

reset_password_otp.page_title: Set New Password - VN Weather Hub
reset_password_otp.title: Set New Password
reset_password_otp.subtitle: Enter a new password for your account

verify_email.title: Email Verification
verify_email.subtitle: One more step to complete your registration!
verify_email.check_inbox: Check your Inbox and Spam folder
verify_email.no_receive: If not received, click Resend OTP

verify_otp.title: OTP Verification
verify_otp.subtitle: Enter the 5-digit code sent to your email

password_reset_sent.title: Email Sent!
password_reset_sent.message: We've sent password reset instructions to your email.
password_reset_sent.check_inbox_desc: The reset link has been sent to the email you entered. Please also check your spam folder if you don't see it.
password_reset_sent.validity_desc: For security reasons, this link expires after 24 hours. If expired, you can request a new one.
password_reset_sent.no_email_desc: Make sure your email is correct and check the spam folder. You can also request a new email.

password_reset_complete.page_title: Password Reset Complete - VN Weather Hub
password_reset_complete.title: Password has been reset!
password_reset_complete.message: Your password has been updated successfully. You can now login with your new password.
password_reset_complete.security_note: Security tip: Don't share your password with anyone and change it periodically.

train.title: TRAIN ML MODEL
train.subtitle: Select dataset, configure model and start training. Results will be saved to Machine_learning_artifacts/latest/
train.guide_step1_desc_p1: Choose data folder and file from the dropdown list.
train.guide_step1_desc_p2: Or scroll down to "Available datasets" table and click "✅ Use" button for quick selection.
train.guide_step1_desc_p3: Recommended to use cleaned data (Cleaned Merge) for best results.
train.guide_step2_desc_quick: ⚡ Quick config: Select model type (Ensemble, XGBoost, LightGBM, CatBoost or Random Forest), adjust test/validation ratio
train.guide_step2_desc_advanced: 🔧 Advanced config: Edit JSON config directly, full parameter control
train.guide_step2_desc_p2: Check "Use default config" to use config from train_config.json file.
train.guide_step3_desc_p1: Click "🚀 Start Training". Monitor progress via progress bar and detailed logs.
train.guide_step3_desc_p2: When complete, result metrics (R², RMSE, MAE) will appear in "Latest Training Results" section.
train.tips_tip1: ⭐ Ensemble (Recommended) combines multiple models (XGBoost, LightGBM, CatBoost) for improved accuracy and stable forecasting
train.tips_tip2: Default target column is rain_total - change if forecasting different variable
train.tips_tip3: Test size = 0.15 and Valid size = 0.15 are recommended configurations
train.tips_tip4: Check Overfit Status in Diagnostics — "GOOD" status is ideal
train.tips_tip5: Rain Detection Accuracy indicates rain/no-rain classification accuracy
train.optuna_desc: Run Optuna to find best hyperparameters for Ensemble model. Results will be automatically saved to best_params_ensemble.json.
train.optuna_auto_apply: Automatically apply best params to train_config.json after optimization
train.config_desc: Select dataset, model type and parameters. Or use default config.
train.model_recommend: 💡 Recommended: Use Stacking Ensemble for the highest accuracy
train.forecast_horizon_hint: Number of hours into the future the model will forecast. Default: 24h.
train.sort_by_time_label: Sort by time (sort_by_time) — recommended for time series data
train.config_json_hint: Edit JSON config above. When submitted, this config will override quick configuration.
train.dataset_table_empty: No datasets yet. Please crawl or merge data first.
train.metrics_explain_intro: Use these metrics together to understand model quality instead of relying on a single number.
train.metrics_explain_r2_desc: Explained variance score (closer to 1 is better). Practical rule: >0.80 good, 0.60–0.80 fair, <0.50 needs improvement.
train.metrics_explain_rmse_desc: Root Mean Squared Error. Lower is better and it is more sensitive to large errors (outliers).
train.metrics_explain_mae_desc: Mean Absolute Error. Lower is better and easy to interpret because it shares the target unit.
train.metrics_explain_rain_acc_desc: Rain/no-rain classification accuracy. Often >0.85 is good, >0.90 is very good (depends on class imbalance).
train.metrics_explain_overfit_desc: If TRAIN is much higher than VALID/TEST, the model may be overfitting. Aim for balanced TRAIN, VALID, TEST scores.
train.metrics_explain_zero_ratio_desc: Share of no-rain samples in target. A very high ratio means strong imbalance; monitor Rain Acc/F1 more carefully.
train.metrics_explain_tip: Tip: prioritize TEST metrics first (generalization), then compare TRAIN–VALID–TEST gaps to detect overfit/underfit.
train.best_params_explain_intro: Best Params are the best hyperparameter set found by Optuna for your selected optimization metric.
train.best_params_explain_trials: 🔁 Trials: number of search attempts. 50–200 is often enough; complex data may benefit from 300+.
train.best_params_explain_metric_value: 🎯 Best value: best score for the target metric. For R²/Rain Acc: higher is better; for RMSE/MAE: lower is better.
train.best_params_explain_learning_rate: 📉 learning_rate: often good in 0.03–0.15. Too high can be unstable; too low usually needs more estimators.
train.best_params_explain_n_estimators: 🌳 n_estimators: typically 200–1000. Higher can improve learning but costs more time and may overfit without regularization.
train.best_params_explain_max_depth_num_leaves: 🧱 max_depth / num_leaves: controls tree complexity. Too large may overfit; common starting points are max_depth 4–10, num_leaves 16–128.
train.best_params_explain_min_child_samples: 🧪 min_child_samples: typically 20–100. Increasing it smooths the model and helps reduce overfitting on noisy data.
train.best_params_explain_subsample_colsample: 🧩 subsample / colsample_bytree: usually 0.6–0.95. Slightly lower values often improve generalization.
train.best_params_explain_reg_alpha_lambda: 🛡 reg_alpha / reg_lambda: regularization strength (often 0–10). Increase for overfitting, decrease when clearly underfitting.
train.best_params_explain_rain_threshold: 🌧 rain_threshold: threshold for rain/no-rain in stage-1 classification. Higher means fewer rain predictions; lower is more sensitive to rain events.
train.best_params_explain_predict_threshold: 🎚 predict_threshold: probability threshold to convert outputs into rain labels. Tune based on missed-rain vs false-alarm tradeoff.
train.best_params_explain_tweedie_variance_pow: 📐 tweedie_variance_power: often 1.1–1.9 for skewed rainfall distributions. Higher values can fit heavy-tail + many-zero patterns better.
train.best_params_explain_adjust_tip: Quick tuning rule: underfit → increase complexity (estimators/depth) or reduce regularization; overfit → do the opposite and lower learning_rate.
train.stacking_predict_threshold_hint: Rain classification threshold from model output
train.stacking_explain_p1: Stacking Ensemble combines multiple base models (XGBoost, LightGBM, CatBoost, RF) and uses a meta-learner (Ridge Regression) to synthesize results, typically achieving higher accuracy than Ensemble Average.

predict.title: WEATHER FORECAST
predict.subtitle: Use the trained Machine Learning model to forecast rainfall accurately. Supports multiple input methods: dataset, file upload, or manual entry.
predict.guide_step1_desc_p1: Ensure a trained model is available (shown in "Current Model" section).
predict.guide_step1_desc_p2: If not, go to ML Training page to train a model first.
predict.guide_step2_desc_dataset: 📦 From dataset: Select folder → select file → click "Forecast"
predict.guide_step2_desc_upload: 📤 Upload file: Drag & drop or select CSV/Excel file from computer
predict.guide_step2_desc_manual: ✏️ Manual input: Enter weather parameters directly to forecast single point
predict.guide_step3_desc_p1: Results show preview table with y_pred column as forecast values.
predict.tips_tip1: Use "🔮 Use" button in datasets table below for quick dataset selection
predict.tips_tip2: Set max rows = 0 to forecast all data
predict.tips_tip3: For manual input, system auto-calculates average from min/max
predict.tips_tip4: Rain classification threshold (predict_threshold) optimized by Optuna — see "Predict Threshold" in model info
predict.upload_text: Drag & drop file here or click to select
predict.manual_intro: Enter weather parameters to forecast rainfall for single data point.
predict.forecast_now_desc: System will automatically collect latest weather data from all stations, then run the AI model to forecast.
predict.forecast_now_horizon: Model will forecast rainfall based on current weather data.
predict.forecast_now_note: Process may take a few minutes due to crawling data from weather API.
predict.model_selector_recommend: 💡 Recommended: Auto mode will prioritize the most accurate model
predict.model_auto_desc: Prefer Stacking, fallback to Ensemble Average

datasets.title: DATA MANAGEMENT
datasets.empty_clean_merge: No cleaned file for MERGE group.
datasets.empty_clean_raw: No cleaned file for OUTPUT group.
datasets.empty_cleaned: NO CLEANED DATA. CLICK "🧹 CLEAN DATA" TO START.
datasets.clean_modal_sub: Select source → select file → view progress → download result
datasets.clean_merge_choice_desc: Read from Merge_data → save to Clean_Data_For_File_Merge
datasets.clean_raw_choice_desc: Read from output → save to Clean_Data_For_File_Not_Merge
datasets.technique_all_desc: Apply all 7 deep cleaning techniques to the selected file.
datasets.merged_sub: Data files that have been merged and stored centrally. Processed data ready for analysis.
datasets.no_merged_file: NO MERGED FILE IN MERGE_DATA/ FOLDER
datasets.process_subtitle: Manage the entire data processing pipeline from collection, merging, cleaning to analysis. All in one interface.
datasets.merge_box_desc: Automatically detect and merge all data files from multiple sources. Supports CSV/Excel… with structure recognition.
datasets.merge_step1: Scan and analyze files in OUTPUT
datasets.clean_box_desc: Apply cleaning techniques: missing, duplicates, outliers, normalize, encode, datetime, format…
datasets.docs_box_desc: View processing workflow, applied techniques, specifications and usage guide.
datasets.merge_section_desc: Merge raw data files in OUTPUT into a combined dataset in MERGE_DATA.
datasets.output_empty: 📭 OUTPUT is empty — nothing to merge.
datasets.clean_section_desc: Data that has been processed, cleaned and normalized from merged files. Ready for advanced analysis and machine learning.
datasets.clean_merged_group_empty: No cleaned file for MERGE group.
datasets.clean_raw_group_empty: No cleaned file for OUTPUT group.
datasets.raw_data_desc: Browse, preview and download recently collected files. Raw data from sources.
datasets.no_merge_data: 📭 NO MERGED DATA. CLICK "🔗 MERGE DATA" BUTTON BELOW.
datasets.docs_overview_desc: The data processing system is designed to manage the entire data lifecycle from collection to analysis.
